summary: R2 addresses the prior architecture objection in shape: packages/ui no longer creates per-map-entity SVG/DOM, apps/web mounts a Pixi-backed MapRenderer surface, and packages/map-renderer owns render-plan, selection hit handling, presentation formulas, and replace-read-model applyDelta/rebuild semantics. Diff scope remains inside allowed paths, with no sim-core, save/schema, Electron IPC, RNG/time, or production dependency expansion. However, acceptance gates still fail: formatting is not clean, strict typecheck fails after the Pixi import/type surface, and the Chromium Playwright test proves the browser-visible Pixi canvas is not mounted. The task cannot be accepted until the renderer-owned visible map works in the browser and required checks pass.

tests_run.result: On feat/m2-client-map. Modified and untracked task files are within allowed implementation/orchestration/test paths.
R2 writer handoff JSON is valid.
1 web Vitest file passed; 3 tests passed.
Vite production build succeeded. Output includes Pixi renderer chunks; no build error.
Architecture boundaries passed; self-test fixture produced 15 expected violations.
Failed at format:check. Prettier reported style issues in tests/ui-m2-district-list.test.tsx, packages/map-renderer/src/index.ts, and packages/ui/src/index.tsx.

Failed strict typecheck. Errors include @webgpu/types conflicts with TypeScript lib.dom declarations, Pixi FederatedPointerEvent missing PointerEvent.persistentDeviceId, and Pixi AbstractBitmapFont distanceField optionality mismatch.
Failed with the same Pixi/@webgpu TypeScript declaration conflicts surfaced through the web package.

1 passed, 1 failed. The M2 map interaction test timed out waiting for locator('.pixi-map__canvas') to have data-renderer-owner='map-renderer'; Playwright error context shows the React shell rendered controls and panels but no Pixi canvas.

decisions: REQUEST_CHANGES: The prior architectural ownership issue is conceptually addressed, but R2 cannot be accepted while required formatting/typecheck/E2E gates fail.
The remaining blocker is implementation correctness, not a new ADR question: keep the MapRenderer/Pixi ownership direction, but make it typecheck and mount successfully in Chromium.
The protocol-shaped M2 client fixture remains a known non-blocking risk pending live worker query integration; it is not the reason for this request-changes result.
risks: Direct default Pixi import currently exposes WebGPU/type declarations that conflict with the repository's TypeScript/lib.dom setup and may also be pulling non-baseline renderer chunks. The frozen baseline is PixiJS with WebGL2 as the conservative target.

The web shell currently does not surface mount failure; mountPixiMapRenderer can fail silently from the user's perspective because the promise is fire-and-forget in the React effect. Add a deterministic error/reporting path or test-visible failure mode while keeping runtime errors outside authoritative simulation state.
requested_action: Fix R2 before acceptance: 1. Run formatting or manually apply equivalent formatting so pnpm check reaches lint/typecheck. 2. Resolve the Pixi/@webgpu TypeScript declaration conflicts without weakening project strictness or hiding failures; prefer a narrow import/configuration that preserves the WebGL2-baseline Pixi map renderer and avoids unneeded renderer/type surface. 3. Fix the browser mount path so .pixi-map__canvas is actually appended in Chromium and the Playwright map interaction test passes. Capture and expose mount failures in a controlled way instead of silently leaving an empty map surface. 4. Keep React as UI intent/DOM owner only; do not reintroduce SVG/DOM per map entity or duplicate presentation formulas in packages/ui. 5. Re-run at least pnpm check, pnpm build:web, pnpm test:e2e --project=chromium, pnpm --filter @monsoon/map-renderer test, pnpm --filter @monsoon/ui test, pnpm --filter @monsoon/client-core test, task validation, handoff validation, and git diff --check in the next handoff.
